title: Asset sourcing
analysis:
Task 21 implements Stage 6 (Asset Sourcing) of the autopilot-video pipeline. The EDL generated in Stage 5 contains placeholder entries in `music`, `voiceovers`, and `broll_requests` arrays. This task creates four sourcing modules (music.py, broll.py, voiceover.py, fetch_list.py) plus a resolution orchestrator (resolve.py) that processes an EDL, attempts to resolve each asset request, and updates the EDL with resolved file paths.

The EDL structures are well-defined from the edit_planner prompt tool definitions:
- music entries: {mood, duration, start_time}
- voiceover entries: {text, start_time, duration}
- broll_requests entries: {description, duration, start_time}

ModelConfig already has tts_engine (kokoro/elevenlabs) and music_engine (musicgen/fetch_list_only). All external dependencies (MusicGen/audiocraft, Kokoro, Pexels API, Pixabay API, Freesound API, ElevenLabs API) will be mocked in tests following the established pattern (sys.modules patching, MagicMock).

I'll define request dataclasses (MusicRequest, BrollRequest, VoiceoverRequest, AssetRequest) in the source __init__.py since they're shared across modules. The resolve.py module orchestrates the full asset resolution pipeline: extract requests from EDL → attempt sourcing → collect unresolved → generate fetch_list → update EDL with paths.

--- POST-REVIEW AMENDMENT ---
Review identified 8 blocking issues across 4 files:
1. resolve.py: db.upsert_edit_plan() called outside `with db:` context manager (silent data loss)
2. broll.py: dl_response.content buffers entire video files in RAM (OOM risk for 50-500 MB files)
3. music.py: MusicGen model reloaded on every invocation (10-30s wasted per call)
4. broll.py: All 4 HTTP requests have no timeout= (indefinite hang risk)
5. music.py: Freesound search and download have no timeout= (indefinite hang)
6. voiceover.py: ElevenLabs POST has no timeout= (indefinite hang)
7. broll.py: Pexels and Pixabay downloads buffer in RAM (same as #2, both sites)
8. music.py: Freesound track name used unsanitized in filename (path traversal risk)

Steps 13-22 address these issues following TDD: write failing test, then fix implementation.

prerequisites.description: Define shared request dataclasses (MusicRequest, BrollRequest, VoiceoverRequest, AssetRequest) in autopilot/source/__init__.py with fields matching EDL tool-use schemas, plus a resolve_status field for tracking resolution state

steps.description: Write tests for source __init__.py: request dataclass imports, field validation (MusicRequest has mood/duration/start_time/resolved_path, BrollRequest has description/duration/start_time/resolved_path, VoiceoverRequest has text/start_time/duration/resolved_path, AssetRequest is a union type alias), and __all__ exports

Implement request dataclasses in autopilot/source/__init__.py: MusicRequest, BrollRequest, VoiceoverRequest dataclasses with fields from EDL schemas + resolved_path: Path | None. Define AssetRequest as Union type. Export in __all__

Write tests for voiceover.py: test VoiceoverError importable, test generate_voiceover signature (text, output_path, config), test kokoro engine path with mocked kokoro module (patches sys.modules['kokoro']), test elevenlabs engine path with mocked requests/httpx, test output .wav file written to output_path, test unknown engine raises VoiceoverError

Implement autopilot/source/voiceover.py: generate_voiceover(text, output_path, config) dispatches to _generate_kokoro() or _generate_elevenlabs() based on config.tts_engine. Kokoro path loads model and generates wav. ElevenLabs path calls API. Returns path to generated .wav. Raises VoiceoverError on failure

Write tests for music.py: test MusicError importable, test source_music signature (request, config, output_dir), test musicgen engine path with mocked audiocraft module (MusicGen.get_pretrained, model.generate, torchaudio.save), test freesound search with mocked requests (successful + empty results), test fetch_list_only config skips generation and returns None, test returns Path to generated/downloaded audio file, test API key from env var

Implement autopilot/source/music.py: source_music(request, config, output_dir) with _generate_musicgen() for local generation and _search_freesound() for API search. MusicGen path uses audiocraft.models.MusicGen. Freesound path uses requests + FREESOUND_API_KEY env var. Returns Path | None

Write tests for broll.py: test BrollError importable, test source_broll signature (request, output_dir), test Pexels API search with mocked requests (returns video results, downloads top-3), test Pixabay fallback when Pexels returns no results, test returns Path to downloaded video, test API keys from env vars (PEXELS_API_KEY, PIXABAY_API_KEY), test returns None when both APIs fail, test rate limit handling

Implement autopilot/source/broll.py: source_broll(request, output_dir) with _search_pexels() primary and _search_pixabay() fallback. Downloads top-3 results from Pexels, falls back to Pixabay. Uses PEXELS_API_KEY and PIXABAY_API_KEY env vars. Returns Path | None

Write tests for fetch_list.py: test generate_fetch_list signature (unresolved, output_path), test writes valid markdown file, test format includes type/description/suggested_sources/priority columns, test handles empty list (writes header-only markdown), test handles mix of MusicRequest/BrollRequest/VoiceoverRequest, test file written to specified output_path

Implement autopilot/source/fetch_list.py: generate_fetch_list(unresolved, output_path) writes a human-readable markdown table listing each unresolved AssetRequest with type, description, suggested sources (per type), and priority. Handles all three request types with appropriate suggested source recommendations

Write tests for resolve.py: test resolve_edl_assets signature (edl, config, output_dir, db), test extracts MusicRequests from edl['music'], BrollRequests from edl['broll_requests'], VoiceoverRequests from edl['voiceovers'], test resolved assets get file paths inserted into EDL entries, test unresolved assets collected into fetch_list.md, test EDL stored back to DB with resolved paths, test full pipeline integration with mocked source modules

Implement autopilot/source/resolve.py: resolve_edl_assets(edl, config, output_dir, db) extracts requests from EDL arrays, calls source_music/source_broll/generate_voiceover for each, updates EDL entries with resolved_path field, collects unresolved requests, calls generate_fetch_list for unresolved, stores updated EDL in DB via db.upsert_edit_plan, and updates narrative status to 'sourced'

FIX db_transaction_protocol_violation: Add test in test_resolve.py (class TestResolveDBIntegration) that uses a file-based CatalogDB (NOT the autocommit catalog_db fixture) to verify resolve_edl_assets wraps DB writes in `with db:` context manager. Test opens a second connection to the same DB file after resolve_edl_assets returns and asserts the edit plan row is visible (proving commit happened). Also test that update_narrative_status('sourced') is called within the same transaction block. Pattern follows test_captions.py TestCaptionDBCommit.

FIX db_transaction_protocol_violation: In resolve.py, wrap the DB persistence block (lines 156-161) in `with db:` context manager: `with db: db.upsert_edit_plan(narrative_id, json.dumps(edl)); db.update_narrative_status(narrative_id, 'sourced')`. This follows the pattern used by every other module (edl.py:238, script.py:412, asr.py:95, etc.).

FIX unnecessary_allocation + resource_exhaustion + missing_timeout in broll.py: Add tests in test_broll.py verifying: (1) TestPexelsSearch.test_pexels_downloads_use_streaming - assert _requests.get() for downloads is called with stream=True, and iter_content(chunk_size=8192) is called on the response instead of .content; (2) TestPexelsSearch.test_pexels_search_has_timeout - assert _requests.get() for the search call includes a timeout parameter; (3) TestPexelsSearch.test_pexels_download_has_timeout - assert download calls include timeout; (4) TestPixabayFallback.test_pixabay_downloads_use_streaming - same streaming assertion for Pixabay; (5) TestPixabayFallback.test_pixabay_requests_have_timeout - timeout on Pixabay search and download calls.

FIX unnecessary_allocation + resource_exhaustion + missing_timeout in broll.py: (1) Change all 4 _requests.get() calls to include timeout=(10, 120) for search calls and timeout=(10, 300) for download calls. (2) Change download calls to use stream=True: `dl_response = _requests.get(url, stream=True, timeout=(10, 300))` then write with `with open(output_path, 'wb') as f: for chunk in dl_response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192): f.write(chunk)`. Apply to both _search_pexels (lines 117,122) and _search_pixabay (lines 186,191).

FIX hot_path_model_loading in music.py: Add test TestMusicGenEngine.test_musicgen_model_cached_across_calls - invoke source_music twice with musicgen engine (using mocked audiocraft), assert MusicGen.get_pretrained is called only once (not twice). Also add test_musicgen_cache_clearable - verify _musicgen_cache can be cleared for test isolation.

FIX hot_path_model_loading in music.py: Add module-level cache `_musicgen_cache: dict = {}` and helper `_get_musicgen_model(name: str)` that checks cache before calling MusicGen.get_pretrained(). In _generate_musicgen(), replace `model = MusicGen.get_pretrained('facebook/musicgen-small')` with `model = _get_musicgen_model('facebook/musicgen-small')`. Export _musicgen_cache in module scope so tests can clear it.

FIX missing_timeout + unsafe_filename in music.py: Add tests in test_music.py: (1) TestFreesoundSearch.test_freesound_search_has_timeout - assert _requests.get() for search is called with timeout parameter; (2) TestFreesoundSearch.test_freesound_download_has_timeout - assert download call includes timeout; (3) TestFreesoundSearch.test_freesound_sanitizes_filename - create a mock Freesound result with name containing path separators and special chars (e.g. 'my/track\\name<>:.wav'), assert the output filename does NOT contain '/', '\\', '<', '>', ':', or null bytes, and the file is written successfully to output_dir (not a subdirectory).

FIX missing_timeout + unsafe_filename in music.py: (1) Add timeout=(10, 60) to _search_freesound search request (line 147) and timeout=(10, 60) to preview download (line 172). (2) Sanitize filename: add `import re` at top, then before building the filename: `safe_name = re.sub(r'[^\w\-. ]', '_', top.get('name', 'track'))[:50]`, use safe_name in `f"freesound_{top['id']}_{safe_name}"`.

FIX missing_timeout in voiceover.py: Add test TestElevenLabsEngine.test_elevenlabs_post_has_timeout - mock requests.post, invoke generate_voiceover with elevenlabs engine, assert _requests.post() was called with a timeout keyword argument (timeout=(10, 120)).
FIX missing_timeout in voiceover.py: Add timeout=(10, 120) to the _requests.post() call at line 152: `response = _requests.post(url, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=(10, 120))`.

design_decisions.decision: Define request dataclasses in autopilot/source/__init__.py rather than a separate types.py
design_decisions.rationale: The request types are small, shared across the source subpackage, and following the project convention of keeping related types near their usage (like config.py has all config dataclasses). The __init__.py already exists empty and is the natural place for shared types within this subpackage.

design_decisions.decision: Create a separate resolve.py orchestrator module instead of putting orchestration in __init__.py
design_decisions.rationale: The resolution logic (extract from EDL → source → update EDL → write fetch_list → persist) is substantial enough to warrant its own module. This follows the separation of concerns pattern seen elsewhere (e.g., edl.py orchestrates LLM calls + validation + persistence). It also makes it independently testable.

design_decisions.decision: Use environment variables for API keys (FREESOUND_API_KEY, PEXELS_API_KEY, PIXABAY_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY) rather than config.yaml
design_decisions.rationale: API keys are secrets that shouldn't be in config files (which are checked into git). Environment variables are the standard approach and align with the existing pattern (anthropic SDK reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env). This also makes testing easier — just mock os.environ.

design_decisions.decision: AssetRequest as Union type alias rather than base class inheritance
design_decisions.rationale: Python Union type (MusicRequest | BrollRequest | VoiceoverRequest) is simpler, more Pythonic for this use case, and avoids unnecessary class hierarchy. The fetch_list.py can use isinstance() checks to determine request type for formatting.

design_decisions.decision: Add resolved_path field to EDL dict entries rather than creating separate resolution mapping
design_decisions.rationale: Mutating the EDL in-place (adding resolved_path to music/voiceover/broll_request entries) keeps the data co-located and is the simplest approach. The PRD states 'Updated EDL with resolved file paths replacing placeholder requests' which implies in-place modification. The updated EDL is then stored back via db.upsert_edit_plan.

design_decisions.decision: Mock all external dependencies at the sys.modules level in tests
design_decisions.rationale: Following the established pattern from test_edl.py, test_asr.py, and test_audio_events.py which mock anthropic, whisperx, panns_inference etc. at sys.modules level. This avoids requiring ML model downloads or API credentials in the test environment.

design_decisions.decision: source_broll downloads top-3 results to disk, returns list of Paths, letting caller select
design_decisions.rationale: The PRD specifies 'Download top-3 results. Let calling code select best match.' The resolve.py orchestrator can then select the first result or implement a selection heuristic. This avoids coupling download logic with selection logic.

design_decisions.decision: Use streaming downloads (iter_content) for all video/audio file downloads in broll.py
design_decisions.rationale: Review found that dl_response.content buffers entire video files (50-500 MB each) in RAM. With up to 3 downloads per B-roll entry across multiple entries, this causes OOM. Streaming with iter_content(chunk_size=8192) keeps memory constant. The voiceover.py ElevenLabs code already uses this pattern correctly.

design_decisions.decision: Cache MusicGen model at module level via _musicgen_cache dict
design_decisions.rationale: Review found MusicGen.get_pretrained() called on every invocation (10-30s per load, ~300 MB). Dict-based cache with _get_musicgen_model() helper allows model reuse across calls within the same process. Tests can clear via _musicgen_cache.clear().

design_decisions.decision: Use tuple timeouts (connect_timeout, read_timeout) for all HTTP requests
design_decisions.rationale: Review found all HTTP calls in broll.py, music.py, voiceover.py had no timeout, risking indefinite hangs. Using (10, 120) for search/API calls and (10, 300) for large downloads follows requests library best practice. Shorter timeouts for search (10, 60), longer for video downloads (10, 300).

design_decisions.decision: Sanitize external filenames with regex before filesystem use
design_decisions.rationale: Review found Freesound track names used unsanitized in filenames. Names with '/' cause path traversal, null bytes cause truncation. Using re.sub(r'[^\w\-. ]', '_', name)[:50] strips all unsafe characters and caps length.

design_decisions.decision: Wrap resolve.py DB writes in `with db:` context manager for atomic commit
design_decisions.rationale: Review found db.upsert_edit_plan() called outside `with db:`, violating CatalogDB contract (documented in db.py lines 11-23). Without the context manager, writes are never committed to SQLite, silently discarding all sourcing results. Every other module uses `with db:` for writes.

reuse.what: ModelConfig dataclass with tts_engine and music_engine fields
reuse.how: Import ModelConfig as the config parameter for source_music() and generate_voiceover(). The engine selection fields (tts_engine: kokoro|elevenlabs, music_engine: musicgen|fetch_list_only) are already defined with validation.

resolve.py uses db.upsert_edit_plan() to store the updated EDL with resolved asset paths, and db.get_edit_plan() to load the EDL for resolution. Also uses db.update_narrative_status() to mark narratives as 'sourced'.
Use the existing catalog_db fixture for integration tests in test_resolve.py that need to seed edit plans and verify DB updates.

reuse.what: sys.modules patching pattern for mocking heavy dependencies
reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: create MagicMock modules, patch sys.modules, then import the module under test. Used for audiocraft (MusicGen), kokoro, and API client mocking.

resolve.py reads these arrays to extract asset requests. The fields map directly to the request dataclasses: music[].mood → MusicRequest.mood, broll_requests[].description → BrollRequest.description, voiceovers[].text → VoiceoverRequest.text.

reuse.where: All autopilot modules (edl.py, script.py, audio_events.py, etc.)
reuse.how: Each new module uses logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) for consistent log output.

reuse.what: Exception class pattern (custom Error subclass per module)
reuse.how: Define MusicError, BrollError, VoiceoverError following the same pattern: simple Exception subclass with docstring, listed in __all__.

reuse.what: File-based CatalogDB commit verification pattern from test_captions.py
reuse.how: For testing `with db:` usage in resolve.py: create file-based CatalogDB (not autocommit fixture), run resolve_edl_assets, then open second connection to verify data was committed. Directly follows the pattern from test_captions.py lines 1257-1308.

reuse.what: ElevenLabs streaming download pattern already in voiceover.py
reuse.how: The ElevenLabs code already uses `for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024): f.write(chunk)`. Apply the same streaming pattern to broll.py downloads (with chunk_size=8192 for larger video files).
